rule: Response-weighted analytical gradients are not reliable proxies for MI direction
Analytical gradient magnitude is too weak for beta/gamma without 100-1000Ã— learning rate boost
No local proxy for beta/gamma exists (7 tested, best r=0.44, none >0.7)
Finite-diff MI gradient violates Principle II (external measurement, not self-generated)
Beta/gamma MI landscape has multiple local maxima (no unique optimum)
Per-cell decomposition of global coupling parameters destroys performance (53% MI loss)
Beta/gamma coupling is fundamentally global, not locally decomposable
Multiplicative self-referential meta-rates cause bang-bang oscillation (inherently unstable)
Stage 3 meta-rate must be EXTERNAL to eta, not eta itself
5-seed validation is insufficient for detecting parameter effects in this system
Per-cell eta adaptation does not improve performance â€” the system is insensitive to learning rate heterogeneity
resp_z derivative tower collapses at order 1 â€” higher-order derivatives cannot drive adaptation
Stage 3+ requires a fundamentally different signal family, not derivatives of resp_z
Evaluation protocol CV=29% â€” need 50+ seeds for meaningful comparisons or variance-reduction redesign
Future experiments must use n_perm=8, n_trials=6 (2Ã— exposure) to achieve CVâ‰ˆ20%
Within-seed paired design increases variance â€” do not use
Adding extreme K values (K=3,12) increases variance â€” stick to K=[4,6,8,10]
Evolutionary search with 3-seed eval is unable to find genuine improvements â€” selects for noise
Future search must use 10+ seeds with 2Ã— exposure protocol
delta_correlation one-way eta adaptation actively harms performance - successful adaptation is misread as degradation
delta_stability Phase 2 improvement (+0.0068) is within noise floor - Phase 3 with 10+ seeds required to confirm
One-way eta reduction based on error-push correlation is an anti-signal for this system
Plasticity threshold is non-binding â€” the system is insensitive to threshold variation across [0.01, 0.1]
Phase 1 diagnostics using 3 independent seeds per condition cannot detect effects reliably at CV=37% â€” always use paired seeds or 10+ seeds
alpha_clip_lo/hi are non-binding â€” structurally active (15% saturation) but performance-insensitive across meaningful range
Parameter activity (hitting bounds) does not imply parameter binding (performance depends on value)
Single-K diagnostics insufficient; multi-K protocol required (K=[4,6,8,10] averaging achieves CVâ‰ˆ5.1% vs K=6-only CVâ‰ˆ14.8%)
c028: Always use per-cell cosine similarity for MI gap measurement, not centroid. Centroid can give opposite rankings.
c029: Entry 042 d=7.583 for VERY_NARROW clip bounds is protocol-specific to the short (50-step) protocol and does not generalize to the canonical harness.py protocol
RETRACT c028: per-cell and centroid cosine give identical rankings under the same protocol; neither is inherently superior
Tau's MI effect is K-dependent (positive at K=4, reverses at K=8) â€” no consistent adaptive direction exists

c031: Tau is non-binding in [0.2, 0.5] range. Entry 046 d=+1.202 is a 5-seed false positive (10-seed: d=+0.317, p=0.317). Adaptive tau (plast-driven [0.15, 0.35]) produces d=-0.039 vs fixed. No tau value or adaptive rule improves performance.
c032: Plast-driven per-cell adaptive tau does not beat fixed baselines. Mechanism works (tau values adapt, non-degenerate) but performance is flat â€” tau is not a binding constraint.
c034: Eps is non-binding in [0.05, 0.5] range. Entry 046 eps=0.05 d=-3.092 is a 5-seed false positive (10-seed: d=-0.461, p=0.145). No eps value significantly beats canonical.

c035: Delta optimal at boundary (1.0). Improvement is monotonic: higher delta = better MI. Canonical delta=0.35 is suboptimal by 6%. This is calibration, not an adaptive opportunity - optimum at boundary means adaptive delta cannot beat best fixed value.

c036: State-output divergence is an anti-signal for delta adaptation. Divergence measures per-step state change magnitude; delta performance depends on per-sequence signal fidelity. These are uncorrelated. Converges to ~0.16 regardless of initialization, significantly worse than fixed delta=1.0 (d=-2.4, p=0.0000). Never retry divergence-based adaptive delta.

c037: Architecture ceiling declared. Living Seed frozen frame minimum: 6/8. Substrate cannot satisfy Stage 7 necessary conditions (no self-representation mechanism). Stage progression halted under Amendment 2. Substrate requires architectural modification to proceed.
ANIMA Stage 7 requires hypernetwork H within I generating I's own update weights â€” do not attempt Stage 7 without this mechanism
Self-modification in ANIMA must apply at cycle boundaries (T consolidation points), not per-step â€” prevents c008-analog instability
Stage 7 exit criterion requires novel-input comparison, not training-input comparison â€” must test on held-out sequences
ANIMA Stage 7 self-modification must apply at T cycle boundaries, not per-step â€” prevents c008-analog bang-bang instability
ANIMA validation protocol must characterize CV before setting minimum seed count â€” do not assume Living Seed CV=29% applies
ANIMA evaluation must guard against false positives (c010 lesson) and anti-signals (c020/c036 lesson) even though the specific Living Seed signals are not applicable
c044: ANIMA w_lr interior optimum at 0.0003 (10-seed confirmed). Canonical w_lr updated from 0.01 to 0.0003. Improvement: 3.5Ã— MI gap.
c045: ANIMA tau non-binding in [0.1, 0.7] range. Consistent with Living Seed c032. Do not attempt tau adaptation.
c046: ANIMA gamma boundary-optimal (monotonically increasing to 3.0). Calibration-only, not adaptive opportunity.

c047: Per-step adaptation signals (mean_abs_err, gradient EMA) cannot capture sequence-level w_lr optimality in ANIMA. The MI gap is determined over full K-signal sequences (~1000 steps); per-step signals encode phase presence, not optimal w_lr direction. This is the ANIMA analog of c036 (per-step vs sequence-level timescale gap).

c048: ANIMA Stage 2 vacuous. No Principle-II-compliant per-step signal can detect optimal w_lr. The W-I tension creates an inverted-U at the sequence level, but the information about position on that curve does not exist at the per-step timescale.

c049: No combination of I_fast, I_slow, I_curvature, or their ratios can locate the w_lr interior optimum via dual-timescale I. All signals show <25% variation across 100x w_lr range. MI-err structural decoupling empirically confirmed.
c050: W_velocity is monotone increasing in w_lr and is not available as an internal organism adaptation signal. It cannot serve as a Principle-II-compliant Stage 3 signal.

c051: Additive dual-timescale I reduces MI gap for all tau_slow > 0. Boundary-optimal at tau_slow=0.0. Fast I (tau=0.3) is sufficient; slow I adds misleading temporal blurring that degrades sequence discrimination.
Read and write must be one operation
Must not reduce to Hopfield/softmax-attention-only
Must not require separate memory + generation systems
Must not rely on matrix composition through long chains
Must achieve structural zero forgetting
Must work on dense embeddings without per-dataset tuning
Must beat 1-NN readout over same codebook
Current hardware, no external API
Minimal -- expressible in <100 lines
Not a combination of known techniques
Readout signal factors must not anti-correlate (e.g. attn x displacement)
Readout must be input-conditional, not static vector property (coherence, density)
Spawn threshold must be calibrated per feature space (cosine range varies)
CIFAR-100 forgetting is class-incremental interference, not codebook drift
Sum-all aggregation fails when class size >> effective neighbors; only sparse selection (top-k) preserves signal
